stories.body: US Vice President JD Vance and senior Iranian officials including parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi are gathering in Switzerland with Pakistani mediators even as Tehran claims it has shut the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon, a move the US military says has not halted traffic; Iran argues the closure and its demand for a Lebanon ceasefire stem from alleged Israeli violations of a fresh US-Iran truce, while Washington insists the talks will tackle both nuclear and Lebanon ceasefire issues under a 60-day timeline for a final deal.
stories.headline: US and Iran resume Swiss talks amid disputed Strait of Hormuz closure

stories.body: Health officials in Gaza say six people, including Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah and at least one child, died in Israeli strikes on a home in Bureij camp and other sites despite an October ceasefire, with the IDF alleging without evidence that Wishah and the other victims were Hamas operatives while Al Jazeera accuses Israel of systematically targeting journalists and vows legal action amid ongoing reports of civilian casualties and strained humanitarian relief.
stories.headline: Israeli strike kills Al Jazeera cameraman as ceasefire violations mount in Gaza

stories.body: At least 16 people were killed in southern Lebanon after Israeli airstrikes that Israel says responded to Hezbollah projectiles even though a ceasefire with the group had supposedly taken effect, and Tehran is tying progress in its US negotiations to an Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon while reiterating support for Hezbollah as the UN warns of mounting humanitarian risks across the region and maritime tensions flare near the Strait of Hormuz.
stories.headline: Israel-Hezbollah front threatens broader peace push despite declared ceasefire

stories.body: NASA ordered five astronauts into their docked Crew Dragon for about two hours after air leak rates in Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to two pounds of air per day and disagreements arose over Roscosmos plans to saw into the hull, but the agency let crews return once Russian teams paused repairs, underscoring ongoing tensions over how to fix repeated leaks on the 27-year-old orbital lab that has never been evacuated.
stories.headline: NASA backs off ISS evacuation after leak repair dispute with Roscosmos

stories.body: Kyiv’s mayor Vitali Klitschko said large fires broke out in the Podil district and on a nine-story building while cars burned in Obolon as residents sheltered during a heavy Russian missile and drone assault that followed Moscow’s warning of systematic strikes on command centers, prompting President Volodymyr Zelenskiy to urge Ukrainians to heed air raid alerts amid fears of another massive attack.
stories.headline: Kyiv mayor reports one of heaviest Russian barrages in weeks

stories.body: Ousted Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko said on X that his Pastef party, which holds a parliamentary majority, will not join President Bassirou Diomaye Faye’s next government after disagreements over the party’s executive role, a standoff that fuels political instability while Senegal grapples with a misreported-debt scandal that froze a $1.8 billion IMF program and pushed debt to 132% of GDP.
stories.headline: Senegal’s Sonko keeps Pastef out of new government as debt crisis looms

stories.body: President Donald Trump claimed multiple arrests proved saboteurs wrecked the $14 million blue-lining project at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after algae blooms and peeling paint spoiled the renovation, but agencies have not confirmed vandalism and one detained visitor, former Olympic canoeist David Hearn, says he merely touched a loose fragment while workers were already addressing the damage.
stories.headline: Trump blames Reflecting Pool failure on vandals as arrested cyclist denies damage

stories.body: In a 52-47 vote, Senate Republicans advanced a massive immigration enforcement bill without Democratic support and resisted efforts to permanently kill President Trump’s controversial fund to compensate allies who allege government mistreatment, leaving the fund on hold but intact despite GOP defections and warnings from both parties that it resembles a political slush fund as the measure heads to the House.
stories.headline: Senate approves $70 billion ICE boost while keeping Trump’s $1.8 billion ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

stories.body: Councilmember Janeese Lewis George defeated Kenyan McDuffie in the Democratic primary, effectively securing the Washington, D.C., mayor’s office given the lack of serious November opposition, and she is promising to unwind Trump-era cooperation between city police and federal immigration agents, pursue social housing and universal child care, and resist any future federalization of the Metropolitan Police Department.
stories.headline: Janeese Lewis George wins DC mayoral primary on anti-Trump platform

stories.body: Investigating magistrate Juan Carlos Peinado ordered Pedro Sánchez’s wife Begoña Gómez, her Moncloa adviser Cristina Álvarez, and businessman Juan Carlos Barrabés to stand trial on charges including influence peddling, corruption, malfeasance, and embezzlement, while imposing travel bans and biweekly court check-ins on Gómez and Álvarez after accepting a far-right group’s request for the measures due to alleged flight risk tied to her presidential spouse’s waning mandate.
stories.headline: Judge Peinado sends Begoña Gómez to trial and seizes her passport

stories.body: La Moncloa denounced Judge Peinado’s order as politically motivated persecution after he suggested the National Police officers guarding Begoña Gómez could help her flee, prompting Interior Minister Fernando Grande-Marlaska to file a formal complaint with the General Council of the Judiciary accusing the magistrate of gravely questioning the legality and professionalism of state security forces and demanding corrective measures.
stories.headline: Spanish government accuses Peinado of persecuting Gómez and defaming police escorts

stories.body: Former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero endured a tense 156-minute interrogation by National Court judge José Luis Calama over consulting fees and alleged luxury meals with businessmen tied to Dubai ventures, with the magistrate repeatedly pressing him about payments from the firm Análisis Relevante and insisting “the data kills the narrative” as he kept the ex-leader under investigation.
stories.headline: Zapatero fails to sway skeptical judge probing consulting payments

stories.body: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez noted wryly that his amnesty law helped the conservative Popular Party recognize Junts as a legitimate actor, and El País reports the PP is now engaging in tactical collaboration with exiled Catalan leader Carles Puigdemont to rebuild bridges with Catalan and Basque conservatives, creating a paradoxical realignment that could undercut Sánchez’s coalition despite being enabled by his reconciliation policies.
stories.headline: PP’s tactical rapprochement with Puigdemont reshapes Spanish right

stories.body: Spain’s meteorological agency Aemet and Catalonia’s Meteocat warn that a blocking ridge and incoming upper-level low are dragging Saharan air northward, pushing highs above 40°C in the Ebro valley and parts of Ponent, triggering orange alerts across nine regions, nights above 25°C along the Mediterranean, and hazy skies over Barcelona as the astronomical summer begins.
stories.headline: Catalonia braces for season’s first major heatwave with Saharan air mass

stories.body: After four drownings, three fatal, within hours on Catalan beaches including Barcelona’s Sant Miquel, aquatic safety expert Ramsès Martí told betevé the 25-year-old lifeguard system lacks rules on staffing ratios, surveillance zones, or equipment, warning that overcrowded beaches before and after official patrol hours and stretches of 80,000 square meters monitored by a single guard make preventive vigilance impossible and demand an urgent policy update.
stories.headline: Spike in drownings renews calls to overhaul Catalan lifeguard model

stories.body: At the Ateneu del Clot, the Assemblea Educativa de Catalunya resolved to stage demonstrations during local festivals this summer and ensure the 2026–27 school year starts with strikes and mobilizations targeting Education Minister Esther Niubó, with unions now tasked with formal strike calls as members debate whether to pursue indefinite, regional, or staggered stoppages after rejecting a preliminary labor accord.
stories.headline: Teachers’ assembly plans summer protests and disruptive strikes for next school year

stories.body: Bisbal d’Empordà residents were without running water for a day after a main near Carrer Canigó ruptured at 3:30 a.m., and city crews had to reopen a trench to replace an additional meter of pipe before fully restoring service at 4 a.m.; Mayor Òscar Aparicio blamed five decades of deferred maintenance and noted the municipality has just tendered a long-planned replacement of the problematic line following a protracted legal fight with former operator Sorea.
stories.headline: Bisbal d’Empordà restores water after 24-hour outage highlights aging pipes
